title: Alyxia suavis

The classification of Alyxia suavis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its distinct characteristics. As a member of the genus Alyxia, Alyxia suavis is one of several species within this group.

intro: Alyxia stellata is a plant species also known as Alyxia suavis, which is a flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The health benefits of Alyxia suavis include its potential use as an antimalarial agent, as well as its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Alyxia suavis include alkaloids, particularly stellatine and stellaticine, which have been shown to possess various pharmacological activities. Traditionally, Alyxia suavis is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
